field.name: scp_id
field.help: SCP managing this host configuration. Auto-register sets this automatically; manually assign to enable SCP synchronization for manually-created hosts.

field.name: scp_sync_enabled
field.help: Enable synchronization with the assigned SCP.

field.name: scp_last_sync_ts
field.help: Timestamp of last successful SCP synchronization

field.name: scp_setup_in_progress
field.help: True while async auto-register setup is running

field.name: scp_hash
field.help: Last hash received from SCP for change detection

div.h4: Enabling SCP Sync for Manually-Created Hosts
div.p: For hosts created manually in Sunray (not via auto-register), you can enable synchronization with an SCP to keep users and access rules in sync.

ul.li: The host must exist in the SCP's database or configuration
The SCP must return this host's FQDN in its response when called

ol.li: Create the protected host normally in Sunray (name, domain, backend_url, worker)
Select the SCP from the scp_id dropdown
Verify with the SCP admin that the host FQDN is included in the SCP's response
The periodic sync cron (every 5 minutes) will pick up the host and synchronize users and access rules

div.p: Users and access rules will be kept in sync with the SCP on every polling cycle. You can disable sync at any time by unchecking scp_sync_enabled without affecting the host.
